Wait, let me recalculate. The right green started at [30,27]-[32,29], center at col 28. After 6 RIGHT actions (each moving 3 cells), it should be at col 28 + 18 = 46. But it's at col 31, which is 28 + 3 = 31. So it only moved 1 RIGHT action worth (3 cells), not 6.

- Initial position: [30,27]-[32,29], center at col 28

So the right green only moved 3 cells right, not 18 cells. This means the RIGHT actions didn't all execute, or the green square stopped moving after 1 action.

sections.content:

<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Right green is at [30,30]-[32,32], need to get it to right panel
# Try selecting it and moving RIGHT again
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 31, 'col': 31}])

# Try RIGHT

lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"Bar W={lines[63].count('W')}")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"Green: boundary={n['boundary']}")
    if n['pixels'] == 1:
        print(f"Center: color={n['color']}, at {n['boundary'][0]}")

# Try UP
action([{'action': 'UP'}])
seg = current_frame.segmentation
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"After UP: bar W={lines[63].count('W')}")

seg = current_frame.segmentation
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# Try LEFT

        print(f"Green: boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
